142.reason: [After detecting "*/", an extra read() is performed without checking if the stream has ended, which may cause curChar to become invalid or skip the next character incorrectly. Also, if the comment is not terminated (i.e., reaches EOF before "*/"), this loop exits but still performs a final read(), potentially causing incorrect state or missing EOF handling.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [After detecting "*/", an extra read() consumes the character following '/', potentially skipping a valid token or causing incorrect line tracking]

204.reason: [When encountering a single '&' or '|', an error should be reported, but the lexer should not generate a valid token like AND or OR; returning a token here incorrectly treats the single character as a valid operator, which violates the specification that such cases are lexical errors and should only produce an error entry without a corresponding valid token.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&' or '|', an error should be reported, but the lexer should not generate a valid token like AND or OR; returning a token here incorrectly treats the single character as a valid operator, which violates the specification that such cases are lexical errors and should only produce an error entry without a corresponding valid token.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&' or '|', an error should be reported, but the lexer should not generate a valid token (like AND/OR) for an invalid single character. Returning a token here incorrectly treats the single '&' or '|' as a valid AND/OR token, which violates the specification that such cases are lexical errors and should only produce an error entry, not a token.]

205.reason: [When encountering a single '&' or '|', it should not produce a valid token of type AND/OR; instead, it should only report an error and skip the invalid character. Returning a token here leads to incorrect output for erroneous input.]
